reviews.review_msg: Casey read off the slides, the information came directly from the TB, and the lectures were three hours. Additionally, the midterm was worth 45% and the final 55% of your grade, which made it challenging to do well in this class if you recieved a bad grade on one or the other.

Casey knew a lot about the course content and I thought she was very nice. Her lectures were interesting to me, though I purposely avoided reading the textbook before lectures because the they were just the chapters with less detail and then I'd read it after class. There were in-class activities, but one non-exam component would have been helpful.
55% of your grade is final exam, 45% is midterm. Half the content from lectures don't match what's tested and were each over 100 questions.

Dr. Kirkpatrick is a very friendly professor. She gives us exam information, such as the types of questions and materials to focus on, in advance and creates fun activities to help us understand the concepts. She also gave me information about the exam schedule when I emailed her about my absence due to a conference (if that info is given).

Every single week we had to read two chapters which TOOK HOURS! I learned ZILCH in lectures. The teacher regurgetated the material she had already given us, and if it was possible to die of boredom, my soul has already left this mortal plane two months past!

The exams were BRUTAL. More than a 100 questions on ancient monkeys! WHY?!
One of my favourite profs. Really interesting and gave all questions that were on midterm and test with a few added. If you put in some time, you got great marks.
Good professor. Interesting anecdotes. Clear explanation. Very demanding. Took his biological anthropology class just to have him teach me again.
Although this class was a later class, Mark kept things fairly interesting. Considering, he had limited people to work with(as he posted his lectures on the web) I'd say he did a good job. Fair exams
So boring! Could not pay attention during class due to a drab monotone voice that did not peak my interest whatsoever.

He made me hate archaeology. Stay away from this prof if you can. Utter snob and very rude when you approach him for help. Why be a prof if you're going to be a jackass? At least the TA was nice and helpful.
Legend

If u want to experience the true the Definition of inconvenience, please take this course 100%. U will be blown away by how much she loves to ignore ur emails, not post grades till whenever she feels like posting, and give u lower mark on ur final grade than what u have. Unacceptable amount of inconvenience and lack of communication 1 after another
Good Prof, kind and respective. Her course needs to be a little easier in terms of course outline but overall she is solid
Best prof I've had so far at Sfu. Easy class if you keep up with lectures and readings.

Most disorganized prof I ever had at SFU. She made ARCH 131 a nightmare. The course was messy where some lectures would not play and were posted randomly and not consistent with the syllabus laid out. The quizzes would have questions that weren't even related to what we were learning for the week. She and the TAs barely responded to emails.

Dennis is a solid, approachable professor and he often brings fossils which makes the lectures interactive. Course is 45% midterm and the rest is based on your final. Questions are super super specific which can drop ur grade quite fast. NOT an easy elective so dont come in with that expectation
Absolute Legend! If you watch lectures and take notes, you will do well in the class.

He speaks very fast on the online videos, I used 0.75 speed and paused it often. You have to take good, detailed notes, sometimes he will say something but not write it on the slides and it ends up being on the quiz or exam. Some interesting content, but a lot of memorization of details like cave sites and physical traits.

I would not recommend this class as an elective. Exam was difficult and very specific to his lectures and opinions which are boring. Need to spend hours studying content. Recommend taking other courses rather than this class

Amazing prof! Super approachable and easy to understand and keeps student interest constant in 3 hour lectures which is rare. The course is based 45% on the midterm and the rest is based on the final. Not exactly the easiest course bec exams are super detailed.

I took this class to fulfill my science requirement as a crim student, and it was not what I was expecting based on reviews. The quizzes and exams were online but closed book with very specific questions (e.g., asking you to know how to differentiate between different subspecies of humans). Also spent a week on primates. Take at your own risk
Professor Dennis runs a tough but rewarding class. Lectures are dense - take detailed notes and really study to ace the exams. Unstuck study helped me prepare, along with the free textbook.

If you want to do well you have to make flashcards, this is 100% a memorization course. Do the readings as there is other testable information in there that isn't mentioned in the lectures. He is very funny and often goofs around in his lecture videos -- which is appreciated.

Lots of material covered in lectures. Regular revision is important. Everyone who complains about reviewing long lectures didn't give themselves enough time to study. If you can visualize a general timeline and understand, not just memorize the information you'll do very well. Dennis is a great prof, material is cool and he often brings artifacts.

This is the class that made me switch my major to Archaeology. Dennis is an amazing professor and lecturer, but make sure to pay close attention to the lectures and take notes. The course is graded only by a few quizzes, a midterm and final, and a group project.

Your grade is determined by 2 exams worth about ~50% each. Take very detailed notes during the lectures (not just what's on the slides) and memorize as much as you can. His exams focus on specific facts, rather than overall understanding of concepts. Don't take this class if your memory is bad.

HORRIBLE PROFESSOR AND I HATED THIS CLASS. Boring and 15 lectures that are 2-3 hours long each! All the notes from the second half of the lectures are 65 PAGES. No words on the slides, only a photo, he is so boring, monotone, robotic. 2 exams, 2 discussions, 3 quizzes. Not specific on what he wants. DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS PLEASE.
this final broke me in ways i have never been broken before.

two exams for the whole semester that will make the archeological trenches your GRAVE! if you were sick and couldn't go to lecture GOOD LUCK cuz the slides are so unbelievably HORRID! pictures will cover the text in the slides so you can't read ANYTHING and only provides lecture audio that cuts out half way through the lecture. never again...

Interesting class, but grades ONLY based on the midterm and final. If you are a neurodivergent student that struggles with memorization I would NOT recommend taking this course. He will NOT tell you what to study specifically at all, so good luck guessing. Have fun memorizing fourteen 3 hour lectures and hoping you remember enough to pass

Took this class online and his lecture videos were so boring. It was so obvious that he was reading a script and you could even hear him turn the page in the recordings. Course is only graded by 3 quizzes, 2 exams, and 2 discussions. Lots of memorization needed but if you grind hard you can get a good mark. Not a lot of work though imo.
Only graded on 2 exams and you have to memorize every single word from each lecture. Lots of memorization and you need to go to lecture to take notes.
Really interesting lectures and be sure to memorize every word of the slides for the tests, especially when it comes to hominins

An inspiring  movie or documentary before most of the lectures.The tests are quite manageable, if you pay attention to the lecture slides, and also to the textbook. At the end of the course, he curved up the final exam a lot. Nice prof.

You're marked on 2 exams and 1 small paper. 1/3 of lectures have a BBC video, sometimes with David Attenborough. He says that exam content is focused on concepts rather than random facts but he didn't do that with the final exam. Thankfully, he bumped everyone's final exam mark by 14%. Pay attention in class and take notes and you'll be fine.
He is a funny and geed prof, however, the midterm exam and the final are super hard, you must read the textbook and pay attention to lectures in order to pass this course.

I took ARCH 131 with Mark last Summer to fulfill a Breadth requirement. Hes an awesome prof. Very funny and personable. I have now fulfilled an extended minor in Arch because Mark made me want to continue taking Arch classes. The course was comprised of 4 exams, all multiple choice. Go to lectures and study and youll do well.

Mark is a really good prof and I don't really see why there are negative reviews on him. I took this course as intersession and Mark makes the 3hour lectures quite bearable. The exams are so simple and he gives up to 10 marks of bonus on the final. Very engaging and approachable.

he's a super funny guy, really nice, and you could tell he was really passionate about his field.. but the class was REALLY hard.. i took it as an elective and regret it.. the exams were super hard and there was a TON of readings. thank god he scaled the exams or else i probably would have failed :/

Mark is obviously very passionate about human origins. I think he would be a great teacher for an upper level course but NOT for a 100 level course. Grading was harsh and tests were hard, each one had to be scaled. If you do take this course GO TO LECTURE and take diligent notes. Don't take this course unless you are interested  in human origins.

This is an example of a prof who shouldn't be teaching 100 level classes. He is obviously very passionate, but impossible to keep up with as a beginner, and his tests/marking rhetoric are very hard and inconsistent. The course was trying to do too much, and in the process he has likely turned a lot of eager students off of human origins.

I strongly, STRONGLY, advise others not to take this course. Even when you study hard it's extremely difficult to get a good grade. It was never clear what we should study. Some questions on the exams seemed to come out of nowhere, and thats WITH studying my butt off!  Very difficult, certainly challenged me...
Amazing professor, very sweet and really cares about his students. His classes do require you to read a lot but they're very interesting and you should definitely take ARCH100 or 131 as an elective.
Such an amazing professor, One of the best in the Arch department. He truly cares about his students and his lectures are very interesting as he covers in-depth analysis on almost all topics.

It took me 15 minutes to watch 5 minutes of a lecture because of how hard it is to understand her. You can imagine how long it took to watch an entire lecture. Would be cool if she had captions but nope! Having the final be worth 60% is insane for a 100 level course. She also takes forever to respond to her emails so gl if you need any help.

If you want to understand the course you cant she has an accent and there are 40 different species and all of them sound like shsjskngkajwhjnfk. you spend more time trying to understand a sentence she says than you do studying

Biggest issue is a 40/60 grading system (40 midterm, 60 final) and the fact she tends to do trick Qs on the tests ESPECIALLY for T/F Qs. It's basically just memorization, I studied for 8 hrs the day before the final and 4 hrs the day of and it felt way better than the midterm. At least I found the info interesting or I would have been screwed

A background in biology is needed. ARCH131 with Angela - the worst class if you are looking for easy electives. She disrespects and mocks religion. 150 questions for the final. Every question is super tricky. She gives you a sentence that seems right by 95%, but then adds only one word to make it wrong. She does not test if students have knowledge.

Mark for an A- is 92%, stricter than most courses. Your mark is determined by 2 tests and 3 quizzes. Lectures are mostly borrowed from a previous professor. Information is fairly interesting, with a few documentary films thrown in as class material. Would be a class worth recommending if the grading was not so strict.

The grading system she uses is different from what sfu suggests for some reason, your 91% is treated as a B+!!!! She also takes soo long to respond to your emails sometimes days.   Take Arch 100 or Arch 101 instead, materials are similar but the profs and grading would be better.
Easy course. Strict with everything, bad with emails
